mission.title: seaweedfs: [mount] Fuse Mount seems not writeable through MacOS Finder
mission.description: [mount] Fuse Mount seems not writeable through MacOS Finder. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current seaweedfs deployment
steps.description:
Verify your seaweedfs version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
helm list -n seaweedfs 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working seaweedfs installation.

steps.title: Review seaweedfs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant seaweedfs configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
kubectl get configmap -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=seaweedfs
```
## Bug description
I use `weed mount` on my macOS Developer machine (exact environment description below).

steps.title: Apply the fix for [mount] Fuse Mount seems not writeable through MacOS Finder
steps.description:
…der copy bug on arm64 macOS devices.

# What problem are we solving?
This pull request fixes #2445

# How are we solving the problem?
Moved noapplexattr under an if statement the switches on runtime.GOARCH == "amd64"

# How is the PR tested?
Compiled and tested on macOS Sonoma 14.11 (arm64)

#
```yaml
weed server -dir="./data" --filer=true
weed mount -filer=localhost:8888 -dir=mnt/SeaweedFS -allowOthers -volumeServerAccess=direct -nonempty -replication=000 -filer.path=/
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
kubectl get events -n seaweedfs --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[mount] Fuse Mount seems not writeable through MacOS Finder" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
…der copy bug on arm64 macOS devices.

# What problem are we solving?
This pull request fixes #2445

# How are we solving the problem?
Moved noapplexattr under an if statement the switches on runtime.GOARCH == "amd64"

# How is the PR tested?
Compiled and tested on macOS Sonoma 14.11 (arm64)

# Checks
codeSnippets:
####################################################
# Customizable filer server options
####################################################
[filer.options]
    # with http DELETE, by default the filer would check whether a folder is empty.
    # recursive_delete will delete all sub folders and files, similar to "rm -Rf"
    recursive_delete = false

    ####################################################
    # The following are filer store options
    ####################################################

[leveldb2]
    # local on disk, mostly for simple single-machine setup, fairly scalable
    # faster than previous leveldb, recommended.
    enabled = true
    dir     = "./filerldb2" # directory to store level db files

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with seaweedfs installed or the issue environment reproducible.
